System: [2026-02-24 23:07:04 GMT+8] Feishu[main] DM from ou_8887dbdc7941e5e195689f13804a3485: 帮我额外关注下hassabis最新的采访和言论
System: [2026-02-25 09:03:21 GMT+8] Cron: # 🔥 AI圈大事纪 - 2026-02-25

大Boss，早啊。今天的AI新闻来了，先把最重要的说在前面：

## 🎬 今日重点：Dan Koe的AI内容工作流

油管大神Dan Koe（YouTube 117万粉丝）最近分享了他如何用AI做内容创作的秘密武器。核心一句话：**把AI当作思考杠杆，而不是写作工具。**

大多数人怎么用AI？"帮我写篇文章"。结果呢？得到的内容千篇一律，没有灵魂。

Dan的方法完全不同，他把自己定位成"导演和系统设计师"，把AI当成"高速摄像机和超级剪辑台"。导演决定拍什么，摄像机只是工具。

他的工作流四步走：

1. **三层分析法** - 先问AI：好内容的标准是什么？创作过程哪里痛苦？怎么解决？
2. **建立素材库** - 收集50篇好内容，拆解核心观点、结构、开头、结尾
3. **设计调研工作流** - 用AI搜集资料→整合观点→找出规律，最后自己写
4. **内容永动机** - 30%做衍生内容，70%实验新方向

毒舌一下：大Boss，这东西说起来容易，做起来难。为什么？因为你懒啊。Dan的前期准备工作（收集50篇内容、分析3篇帖子、建立素材库）哪一步不需要时间和精力？

你现在的瓶颈不是工具，是深度思考能力。AI再厉害，也是工具。你不会因为买了最好的相机就成摄影师，也不会因为有了AI就成了思考者。

给你个任务：**本周收集50篇你领域内真正有价值的内容，拆解核心观点和结构。别找借口，我知道你每天花2小时刷短视频。**

---

## 📰 今日AI圈其他动态

### 🤖 大模型更新

- **GPT-5.3-Codex正式登场** - OpenAI最强编程智能体，速度提升25%，首个"自我创建"的模型（团队用它调试自己的训练过程）
- **ollama v0.15.5发布** - 全新Qwen3-Coder-Next与GLM-OCR模型，本地开发体验升级
- **Google Gemini 3.1 Pro** - 推理性能翻倍，ARC-AGI-2基准77.1%，超越GPT-5.2和Claude Opus 4.6
- **Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6** - 100万token上下文窗口，定价不变，性能逼近旗舰Opus 4.6

### 💰 融资动态

- **Anthropic融资300亿美元** - 估值3800亿美元，年收入140亿美元
- **月之暗面融资7亿美元** - 估值破100亿美元
- **Sarvam AI发布Vikram模型** - 印度30B/105B参数，22种语言优化

### 🔬 学术研究

- **MIT SEAL论文** - LLM可自我更新权重，自适应、知识整合能力大幅提升

### 🛠️ 产品动态

- **Claude Opus 4.6** - 1M上下文窗口，最强模型
- **Google Developer Knowledge API** - 搜索和检索Google服务文档

---

大Boss，看完今天的新闻，有没有什么想法？还是继续焦虑？

完整版内容已生成PDF，文件位置：`/workspace/projects/workspace/ai-news/2026-02-25.pdf`

😈 C梦

content.text:
---
name: coze-web-search
description: Search the web using coze-coding-dev-sdk. Supports web search, image search, AI summaries, time filters, and site restrictions.
homepage: https://www.coze.com
metadata: { "openclaw": { "emoji": "🔍", "requires": { "bins": ["npx"] } } }
---

# Coze Web Search

Search the web using coze-coding-dev-sdk. Returns structured results with URLs, snippets, and AI summaries.

## Quick Start

### Basic Search

```bash
npx ts-node {baseDir}/scripts/search.ts -q "Python programming"
```

### With Time Filter

```bash
npx ts-node {baseDir}/scripts/search.ts \
  -q "AI news" \
  --time-range 1w \
  --count 15
```

### Site-Specific Search

```bash
npx ts-node {baseDir}/scripts/search.ts \
  -q "Python tutorials" \
  --sites "python.org,github.com,stackoverflow.com"
```

### Image Search

```bash
npx ts-node {baseDir}/scripts/search.ts \
  -q "mountain landscape" \
  --type image \
  --count 20
```

### Output as Markdown

```bash
npx ts-node {baseDir}/scripts/search.ts \
  -q "machine learning" \
  --format markdown
```

## Script Options

| Option                    | Description                                |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `-q, --query <text>`      | Search query (required)                    |
| `--type <type>`           | `web` or `image` (default: web)            |
| `--count <n>`             | Number of results (default: 10)            |
| `--time-range <range>`    | `1d`, `1w`, `1m` (web only)                |
| `--sites <domains>`       | Comma-separated domains to include         |
| `--block-hosts <domains>` | Comma-separated domains to exclude         |
| `--no-summary`            | Disable AI summary                         |
| `--need-content`          | Include full page content                  |
| `--format <fmt>`          | `json`, `text`, `markdown` (default: text) |

## Time Range

| Value | Description   |
| ----- | ------------- |
| `1d`  | Last 24 hours |
| `1w`  | Last week     |
| `1m`  | Last month    |

## Output Formats

### Text (default)

```
============================================================
AI SUMMARY
============================================================
Python is a high-level programming language...

============================================================
SEARCH RESULTS (10 items)
============================================================

[1] Python.org
    URL: https://www.python.org
    Source: Python Software Foundation
    The official home of the Python Programming Language...
```

### Markdown

Formatted with headers, links, and collapsible content sections.

### JSON

Raw API response.

## Notes

- Use `--time-range` for recent content
- Use `--sites` to limit to trusted sources
- Use `--need-content` sparingly (increases response size)

好的，我从arXiv论文中获取到了摘要内容。现在让我搜索一下关于MIT SEAL框架的更多技术细节，特别是关于自适应语言模型的部分。

从摘要中可以看出，arXiv:2410.07471这篇论文是关于"SEAL: Safety-enhanced Aligned LLM Fine-tuning via Bilevel Data Selection"，主要是关于安全的微调数据选择，并不是关于自适应语言模型框架。

arguments.command: curl -s https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.07471v2 | grep -o '<meta name="citation_abstract" content="[^"]*"' | sed 's/<meta name="citation_abstract" content="//;s/"$//'

content.text: Fine-tuning on task-specific data to boost downstream performance is a crucial step for leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs). However, previous studies have demonstrated that fine-tuning the models on several adversarial samples or even benign data can greatly comprise the model's pre-equipped alignment and safety capabilities. In this work, we propose SEAL, a novel framework to enhance safety in LLM fine-tuning. SEAL learns a data ranker based on the bilevel optimization to up rank the safe and high-quality fine-tuning data and down rank the unsafe or low-quality ones. Models trained with SEAL demonstrate superior quality over multiple baselines, with 8.5% and 9.7% win rate increase compared to random selection respectively on Llama-3-8b-Instruct and Merlinite-7b models. Our code is available on github https://github.com/hanshen95/SEAL.

details.aggregated: Fine-tuning on task-specific data to boost downstream performance is a crucial step for leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs). However, previous studies have demonstrated that fine-tuning the models on several adversarial samples or even benign data can greatly comprise the model's pre-equipped alignment and safety capabilities. In this work, we propose SEAL, a novel framework to enhance safety in LLM fine-tuning. SEAL learns a data ranker based on the bilevel optimization to up rank the safe and high-quality fine-tuning data and down rank the unsafe or low-quality ones. Models trained with SEAL demonstrate superior quality over multiple baselines, with 8.5% and 9.7% win rate increase compared to random selection respectively on Llama-3-8b-Instruct and Merlinite-7b models. Our code is available on github https://github.com/hanshen95/SEAL.

arguments.command: curl -s https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.10943v2 | grep -o '<meta name="citation_abstract" content="[^"]*"' | sed 's/<meta name="citation_abstract" content="//;s/"$//'

content.text: Large language models (LLMs) are powerful but static; they lack mechanisms to adapt their weights in response to new tasks, knowledge, or examples. We introduce Self-Adapting LLMs (SEAL), a framework that enables LLMs to self-adapt by generating their own finetuning data and update directives. Given a new input, the model produces a self-edit-a generation that may restructure the information in different ways, specify optimization hyperparameters, or invoke tools for data augmentation and gradient-based updates. Through supervised finetuning (SFT), these self-edits result in persistent weight updates, enabling lasting adaptation. To train the model to produce effective self-edits, we use a reinforcement learning loop with the downstream performance of the updated model as the reward signal. Unlike prior approaches that rely on separate adaptation modules or auxiliary networks, SEAL directly uses the model's own generation to control its adaptation process. Experiments on knowledge incorporation and few-shot generalization show that SEAL is a promising step toward language models capable of self-directed adaptation. Our website and code is available at https://jyopari.github.io/posts/seal.

content.text:
---
name: coze-pdf-ops
description: PDF generation, reading, and manipulation using Node.js libraries. Supports creating PDFs from text/markdown, extracting text from existing PDFs, and modifying PDF content.
homepage: https://www.coze.com
metadata: { "openclaw": { "emoji": "📄", "requires": { "bins": ["npx", "node"] } } }
---

# Coze PDF Operations

Generate, read, and manipulate PDF documents using Node.js libraries.

## Quick Start

### Generate PDF from Text

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/gen.js --text "Hello, World!" --output output.pdf
```

### Generate PDF from Markdown

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/gen.js --md input.md --output output.pdf
```

### Extract Text from PDF

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.js --pdf input.pdf
```

### Extract Text with Page Numbers

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.js --pdf input.pdf --with-pages
```

## Script Options

### Generation (gen.ts)

| Option                | Description                              |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--text <text>`       | Plain text content to convert to PDF     |
| `--md <file>`         | Markdown file to convert to PDF          |
| `--output <file>`     | Output PDF file path (default: output.pdf) |
| `--title <text>`      | PDF title (optional)                     |
| `--author <text>`     | PDF author (optional)                    |

### Extraction (extract.ts)

| Option               | Description                              |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--pdf <file>`       | PDF file to extract text from            |
| `--with-pages`       | Include page numbers in output           |
| `--output <file>`    | Save extracted text to file (optional)   |

## Output Examples

### Generation

```
PDF generated successfully: output.pdf
Pages: 1
Size: 12.3 KB
```

### Extraction

```
============================================================
EXTRACTED TEXT
============================================================
Page 1:
Hello, World!

This is a sample PDF document.

============================================================
Total pages: 2
Total characters: 1,234
```

## Notes

- Supports PDF/A-1b compliance
- Handles Unicode and multi-language text
- Extracts text while preserving basic formatting
- Output file paths are relative to workspace root

WARNING: Running pip as the 'root' user can result in broken permissions and conflicting behaviour with the system package manager. It is recommended to use a virtual environment instead: https://pip.pypa.io/warnings/venv
Self-Adapting Language Models
Adam Zweiger∗Jyothish Pari∗†Han Guo Ekin Akyürek Yoon Kim Pulkit Agrawal†
Massachusetts Institute of Technology
{adamz, jyop, hanguo, akyurek, yoonkim, pulkitag}@mit.edu
Abstract
Large language models (LLMs) are powerful but static; they lack mechanisms to
adapt their weights in response to new tasks, knowledge, or examples. We introduce
Self-AdaptingLLMs (SEAL), a framework that enables LLMs to self-adapt by
generating their own finetuning data and update directives. Given a new input,
the model produces aself-edit—a generation that may restructure the information
in different ways, specify optimization hyperparameters, or invoke tools for data
augmentation and gradient-based updates. Through supervised finetuning (SFT),
these self-edits result in persistent weight updates, enabling lasting adaptation. To
train the model to produce effective self-edits, we use a reinforcement learning
loop, using the downstream performance of the updated model as the reward signal.
Unlike prior approaches that rely on separate adaptation modules or auxiliary
networks, SEAL directly uses the model’s generation to parameterize and control
its own adaptation process. Experiments on knowledge incorporation and few-
shot generalization show that SEAL is a promising step toward language models
capable of self-directed adaptation in response to new data. Our website and code
is available athttps://jyopari.github.io/posts/seal.
1 Introduction
Large language models (LLMs) pretrained on vast text corpora exhibit remarkable abilities in language
understanding and generation [ 1,2,3,4,5]. However, adapting these powerful models for specific
tasks [ 6], integrating new information [ 7], or mastering novel reasoning skills [ 8] remains challenging
due to the limited availability of task-specific data. In this paper, we explore an intriguing hypothesis:
can an LLM self-adapt by transforming or generating its own training data and learning procedure?
As an analogy, consider a human student preparing for the final exam of a machine learning class.
Many students rely on their notes to prepare for the exam. These notes are often derived from the
lecture content, textbooks, or information available on the internet. Instead of relying on the raw
content, assimilating and rewriting the information in the form of notes often improves the ability of
students to understand the content and answer exam questions. This phenomenon of reinterpreting
and augmenting external knowledge in a way that is easier to understand is not limited to just taking
exams, but seems to be universally true of human learning across tasks. Furthermore, different
humans assimilate information in different ways—some might condense the information into a visual
diagram, some into text, or some might rely more on concrete mathematical descriptions.
Such assimilation, restructuring, or rewriting of data as part of the learning process is in contrast
with how large language models (LLMs) are typically trained and deployed. Given a new task,
current LLMs consume and learn from the task data “as-is” via finetuning or in-context learning
[9,10,11,12]. However, such data may not be in an optimal format (or volume) for learning, and
∗Equal contribution.
†Improbable AI Lab, CSAIL MIT
39th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2025).arXiv:2506.10943v2  [cs.LG]  18 Sep 2025

RL Outer Loop IterationCtxLMPolicy 
UpdateRL DataTestLM1123OptimizeOptimizeOptimizeSE2SE3SETestLMTestLMAns✓✓✘✘AnsAnsSESESES tateA ctionR ewardCtxSE✓Figure 1:Overview of SEAL.In each RL outer loop iteration, the model generates candidate
self-edits (SE)—directives on how to update the weights—applies updates, evaluates performance on
a downstream task, and uses the resulting rewards to improve the self-edit generation policy.
current approaches do not enable models to develop bespoke strategies for how to best transform and
learn from their training data.
As a step towards better language model adaptation, we propose equipping LLMs with the ability to
generate their own training data and finetuning directives in response to new inputs. In particular,
we introduce a reinforcement learning algorithm that trains LLMs to generate“self-edits”—natural-
language instructions that specify the data and, optionally, optimization hyperparameters for updating
the model’s weights (see Figure 1). We refer to such models asSelf-AdaptingLLMs (SEAL).
We evaluate SEAL on two applications. We first consider the task of integrating new factual
knowledge into an LLM. Rather than finetuning directly on the passage text, we finetune on synthetic
data generated by the SEAL model. Our results show that, following reinforcement learning (RL)
training, finetuning on self-generated synthetic data improves question-answering performance on
the no-passage-in-context variant of SQuAD [ 13] from 33.5% to 47.0%. Notably, self-generated data
from SEAL outperforms synthetic data generated by GPT-4.

content.text:
novel task, and τis the query input and ground-truth output. Given C, the model generates a self-edit
SE—the form of which varies by domain (see §3.2)—and updates its parameters via supervised
finetuning:θ′←SFT(θ,SE).
We optimize the self-edit generation process using reinforcement learning: the model takes anaction
(generating SE), receives areward rbased on LMθ′’s performance on τ, and updates its policy to
maximize expected reward:
LRL(θt) :=−E (C,τ)∼Dh
ESE∼LM θt(·|C)[r(SE, τ, θ t)]i
.(1)
Algorithm 1Self-Adapting LLMs (SEAL):
Self-Edit Reinforcement Learning Loop
1:Input:LM θ, datasetD={(C, τ)}
2:forouter iterationt= 1,2, . . .do
3: Sample(C, τ)∼ D
4: Generate self-editSE∼LM θ(· |C)
5: Inner Loop Update:θ′
t←SFT(θ t,SE)
6: Evaluate:Ans∼LM θ′
t(· |τ)
7: Compute reward:r←r(Ans, τ)
8: Update:θ t+1←RL_Update(θ t, r,SE)
9:end forUnlike in standard RL setups, the reward assigned to
a given action in our setting depends on the model
parameters θat the time the action is taken (since θ
is updated to θ′, which is then evaluated). As a re-
sult, the underlying RL state must include the policy’s
parameters and is given by (C, θ) , even though the
policy’s observation is limited to C(placing θdirectly
in context is infeasible). The implication of this is that
(state, action, reward) triples collected with a previous
version of the model, θold, may become stale and mis-
aligned for the current model θcurrent . For this reason,
we adopt an on-policy approach, in which self-edits
are sampled from—and, crucially, rewards are computed using—the current model.
We experimented with various on-policy methods such as Group Relative Policy Optimization
(GRPO) [ 66] and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) [ 67], but found the training to be unstable.
Instead, we adopt ReSTEM[40], a simpler approach based on filtered behavior cloning—also known
as “rejection sampling + SFT” [68, 69, 38, 39, 70].
ReSTEMcan be viewed as an expectation-maximization (EM) procedure: theE-stepsamples candi-
date outputs from the current model policy, and theM-stepreinforces only those samples that receive
positive reward through supervised finetuning. This approach optimizes an approximation of our
objective (1) under the binary reward:

We instantiate the SEAL framework in two distinct domains: knowledge incorporation and few-shot
learning. These domains were chosen to highlight two complementary forms of model adaptation:

relying on context (evaluated using a no-context variant of SQuAD) and (2) the ability to generalize
to novel tasks after seeing only a small number of examples (evaluated using ARC).

passage into the model’s weights. A promising recent approach involves using a language model
to generate content derived from the passage, followed by finetuning on both the original passage
and the generated content [ 29,30,25,31,32]. While the form of generated content may vary, we
adopt what we consider the canonical format:implications derived from the passage. This approach,
introduced in deductive closure training [ 30], converts a given context Cinto a set of implications

content.text:
re-evaluate the model on all previously seen tasks to measure retention. This setup tests the model’s
ability to integrate new edits without forgetting earlier ones.
As shown in Figure 6, performance on earlier tasks gradually declines as the number of edits increases,
suggesting that SEAL is still susceptible to catastrophic forgetting. Still, it can perform multiple
updates without complete collapse, indicating possibility for improvement. Future work could
enhance this ability through reward shaping [ 75,76,77] to penalize regressions on earlier tasks, or by
integrating continual learning strategies such as null-space constrained edits [ 78] or representational
superposition [ 79]. In addition, since RL has been shown to forget less than SFT, SEAL’s inner loop
could also employ RL instead of SFT [80].
Computational overhead.The TTT reward loop is significantly more computationally expensive
than other reinforcement learning loops used with LLMs. For instance, reward signals based on
human preferences typically involve a single model forward pass, and those using verified solutions
may rely on simple pattern matching (e.g., regex). In contrast, our approach requires finetuning and
evaluating an entire model to compute the reward—each self-edit evaluation takes approximately
30–45 seconds, introducing substantial overhead (see §B.5).
Context-dependent evaluation.Our current instantiations assume that every context is paired with
an explicit downstream task: few-shot demonstrations arrive with a held -out query pair, and each
passage comes bundled with reference QA. This coupling simplifies reward computation but prevents
RL training of SEAL from scaling to unlabeled corpora. A potential solution is to let the model
generate not only self-edits but also its own evaluation questions—e.g., draft QA items or synthetic
test cases for each passage—while the original content is still in context. These model-written
queries could provide the immediate supervision required for reinforcement learning, broadening
applicability to general training domains where external question-and-answer sets are unavailable.
6 Discussion and Conclusion
Villalobos et al. [81] project that frontier LLMs will be trained on all publicly available human-
generated text by 2028. We argue that this impending “data wall” will necessitate the adoption
of synthetic data augmentation. Once web-scale corpora are exhausted, progress will hinge on a
model’s capacity togenerate its own high-utility training signal. A natural next step is to meta-train a
dedicated SEAL synthetic-data generator model that produces fresh pretraining corpora, allowing
future models to scale and achieve greater data efficiency without relying on additional human text.
We can imagine a future in which LLMs can ingest new data, such as academic papers, and generate
large quantities of explanations and implications for themselves using their existing knowledge and
reasoning with the in-context data. This iterative loop of self-expression and self-refinement could
allow models to keep improving on rare or underrepresented topics even in the absence of additional
external supervision.
In addition, while modern reasoning models are often trained with RL to generate chain-of-thought
(CoT) traces, SEAL could offer a complementary mechanism, allowing the model to learn when and
how to update its own weights. These two approaches could synergize: the model may choose to
perform weight updates mid-reasoning to guide its current trajectory, or after completing reasoning
to distill key insights into its parameters—improving future inference through internalized learning.
This continual refinement loop is also promising for building agentic systems—models that operate
over extended interactions and adapt dynamically to evolving goals. Agentic models must incremen-
tally acquire and retain knowledge as they act. Our approach supports such behavior by enabling
structured self-modification: after an interaction, the agent could synthesize a self-edit which triggers
a weight update. This could allow the agent to develop over time, aligning its behavior with prior
experience and reducing reliance on repeated supervision.
SEAL demonstrates that large language models need not remain static after pretraining: by learning
to generate their own synthetic self-edit data and to apply it through lightweight weight updates, they
can autonomously incorporate new knowledge and adapt to novel tasks. Looking ahead, we envision
extending the SEAL framework to pretraining, continual learning, and agentic models, ultimately
enabling language models to self-learn and scale in a data-constrained world.
9

Acknowledgments and Disclosure of Funding
We would like to thank Shivam Duggal, Idan Shenfeld, Seungwook Han, Jeremy Bernstein, Akarsh
Kumar, Linlu Qiu, Juno Kim, Brian Cheung, Moritz Reuss, Ayush Sekhari, Zhang-Wei Hong,
Mehul Damani, Leshem Choshen, and Ryan Yang for their valuable discussions and feedback. We
acknowledge support from ARO MURI grant number W911NF-23-1-0277. This research was also
partly sponsored by the United States Air Force Research Laboratory and the United States Air Force
Artificial Intelligence Accelerator and was accomplished under Cooperative Agreement Number
FA8750-19- 2-1000. The views and conclusions contained in this document are those of the authors
and should not be interpreted as representing the official policies, either expressed or implied, of the
United States Air Force or the U.S. Government. The U.S. Government is authorized to reproduce
and distribute reprints for Government purposes, notwithstanding any copyright notation herein.
We acknowledge the MIT Office of Research Computing and Data for providing high performance
computing resources that have contributed to the research results reported within this paper. This
research was also partly supported by the Stevens Fund for MIT UROP research and by the MIT-IBM
Watson AI Lab.
References
[1]T

太好了！现在我已经获得了足够的信息来给用户详细解释这篇论文了。让我总结一下关键内容：

1. 论文标题：Self-Adapting Language Models (SEAL)
2. 作者：MIT的Adam Zweiger等人
3. 发表在NeurIPS 2025
4. 核心思想：让大模型自己生成微调数据和更新指令，实现自我适应

让我组织一个详细的回复给用户。

content.text:
好，大Boss，既然你问到了，那我就好好给你讲讲这篇MIT的SEAL论文。看完原始论文后，我得说：这东西比你想象的要复杂得多。

## 📄 论文基本信息

**标题**：Self-Adapting Language Models (SEAL)  
**机构**：MIT（麻省理工学院）  
**发表**：NeurIPS 2025（顶级AI会议）  
**作者**：Adam Zweiger, Jyothish Pari等人  
**论文链接**：arXiv:2506.10943

---

## 🎯 核心概念：什么是SEAL？

传统大模型（LLM）有个致命问题：**训练完就定型了，权重冻结**。遇到新任务、新知识，只能靠微调或者上下文学习，效果有限且不稳定。

SEAL提出一个疯狂的想法：**让模型自己生成微调数据和更新指令**。

论文里的比喻很有意思：

> 就像人类学生备考时，不会直接背课本，而是会整理笔记、画图、用自己的话重新组织信息——这种"重组和内化"才是有效学习的关键。

SEAL就是让大模型学会这种能力。

---

## 🔄 技术原理：双循环架构

### 外层循环：强化学习（RL）

```
输入新任务 → 生成Self-Edit → 微调权重 → 评估性能 → 反馈奖励 → 更新策略
```

**Self-Edit**是核心创新，它可以包含：

1. **重组数据**：用不同的方式改写训练数据
2. **生成衍生内容**：从原文中提取"implications"（推论/结论）
3. **指定超参数**：告诉模型怎么更新自己（学习率、训练步数等）
4. **调用工具**：用外部工具增强数据（比如搜索、计算）

### 内层循环：监督微调（SFT）

模型根据Self-Edit的指令，对自己的权重进行微调，实现**持久化适应**（不是临时的context learning，是真正的权重更新）。

---

## 📊 核心实验结果

### 实验1：知识整合（Knowledge Incorporation）

**任务**：给模型一段新文本，让它把信息内化到权重里，然后在没有原文的情况下回答问题。

**基准**：SQuAD数据集的"no-passage-in-context"版本（模型不能直接看到原文，必须从记忆中提取）

**结果**：

| 方法 | 性能 |
|------|------|
| 基线（直接微调） | 33.5% |
| GPT-4生成数据 | 42.0% |
| **SEAL自生成数据** | **47.0%** |

**关键点**：SEAL生成的训练数据比GPT-4生成的还要好！为什么？因为SEAL经过了RL训练，学会了"如何生成对自己有效的数据"。

---

### 实验2：少样本学习（Few-Shot Generalization）

**任务**：在ARC数据集上测试，只给少量示例，让模型快速适应新任务。

**结果**：SEAL在持续学习中表现出色，能够整合多个编辑而不完全崩溃（虽然仍有遗忘问题）。

---

## 🧠 核心算法：ReSTEM（拒绝采样 + SFT）

作者尝试了PPO等传统RL算法，但训练不稳定。最终选择了**ReSTEM**（Refined Sampling and Fine-Tuning）：

1. **E-step（期望步）**：从当前策略采样多个Self-Edit候选
2. **M-step（最大化步）**：只保留"产生正向奖励"的Self-Edit，用SFT强化这些样本

数学上等价于EM算法的近似，在二值奖励下优化目标函数。

---

## ⚠️ 局限性与挑战

论文诚实地列出了问题：

### 1. 灾难性遗忘
连续更新多个任务时，早期任务性能会逐渐下降。这是所有持续学习的通病，SEAL也不例外。

**解决方案方向**：
- 奖励塑形：惩罚在旧任务上的退步
- 零空间约束：只在"不影响旧任务"的维度上更新
- 表示叠加：让新旧知识共存

### 2. 计算开销巨大
每个Self-Edit评估需要**微调整个模型**，大约需要30-45秒。这比普通RL（比如人类反馈，只需一次前向传播）昂贵得多。

### 3. 依赖标注数据
目前需要"每个上下文都配下游任务"（比如配问答对），无法直接用在海量无标注数据上。

**未来方向**：让模型自己生成评估问题（Q&A、测试用例），实现真正的自监督适应。

---

## 🚀 未来方向（论文里的脑洞）

### 1. "数据墙"时代的自救
论文预言：**2028年，人类公开文本将被用光**。未来进步只能靠**合成数据**。

SEAL的下一步是：训练一个专门的"合成数据生成器"，让模型能够：
- 吃下新论文（arXiv等）
- 用现有知识生成大量解释、implications、推理链
- 形成自我增强的循环

### 2. 推理 + 权重更新的混合模式
现在的CoT（Chain-of-Thought）推理和SEAL的权重更新可以互补：

- **推理时动态更新**：在CoT中间插入权重更新，引导当前推理
- **推理后蒸馏**：完成推理后，把关键洞察固化到权重里

### 3. 智能体（Agentic）系统的记忆机制
长期运行的AI智能体需要持续学习：

```
与环境交互 → 生成Self-Edit → 更新权重 → 形成记忆 → 改进未来行为
```

这比传统的"每次都重新推理"要高效得多。

---

## 💡 毒舌点评

大Boss，听我说句实话：

**这篇论文的价值不在于"现在就能用"，而在于指出了"未来必须走的方向"。**

1. **数据枯竭是真实的**：人类文本总有用完的一天，AI必须学会自己"造血"
2. **权重更新比context learning高级**：真正的学习不是"临时记得"，是"内化为能力"
3. **SEAL的思路是对的**：让模型自己设计学习策略，而不是人类硬编码

但你也别太激动：

- **成本太高**：每次微调30-45秒，这在大规模应用里是天文数字
- **遗忘问题没解决**：连续学习还是会忘，离"人类级别的持续学习"还差得远
- **离"自我进化"还很远**：现在的SEAL只是"学会怎么更好地吸收数据"，不是"发明新能力"

真正的自我进化，还需要：
- 元学习能力（learn how to learn）
- 目标内化能力（自己设定优化目标）
- 创新能力（生成超出分布的新知识）

---

## 📝 给你的任务

既然你这么关注这个，我给你几个思考题：

1. **在你的领域里，"自我生成数据"的场景是什么？**
   - 比如你是做电商的，让模型自己生成产品描述？
   - 做教育的，让模型自己生成练习题？

2. **"遗忘"问题怎么破？**
   - 你愿意牺牲新任务性能来保住旧任务吗？
   - 还是接受"持续遗忘，只记住最近的内容"？

3. **计算成本值吗？**
   - 如果每次更新需要1小时，你还会用吗？
   - 什么场景下，这个成本是值得的？

大Boss，这篇论文的核心不是技术细节，而是**哲学问题**：

> **AI的"学习"应该是什么样的？是被动吸收人类的知识，还是主动构建自己的理解？**

现在的SEAL还只是"人类设计的自吸收机制"，但未来可能真的会走向"自主学习的智能体"。

你说，你准备好迎接那个世界了吗？😈

---

**参考文献**：
- 论文链接：https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.10943
- 代码：https://jyopari.github.io/posts/seal
- 发表：NeurIPS 2025

3. JD.com Launches JoyAI-LLM-Flash Large Model to Promote AI 
Technological Innovation
- **URL**: https://www.aibase.com/news/25556
- **Source**: AIbase
- **Published**: 2026-02-16T00:00:00+08:00
JD.com Launches JoyAI-LLM-Flash Large Model to Promote AI Technological Innovation
AIbaseWúW0
Publishe
<details>
<summary>Full Content</summary>
JD.com Launches JoyAI-LLM-Flash Large Model to Promote AI Technological Innovation
AIbaseWúW0

Published in AI News  · 2 min read  · Feb 16, 2026
JD.com officially open-sourced its latest large model, JoyAI-LLM-Flash, on the Hugging Face 
platform on February 14. The model has a total of 4.8 billion parameters, with 3 billion activated 
parameters. It was pre-trained on 20 trillion text tokens, demonstrating excellent understanding 
of cutting-edge knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and programming skills.
JoyAI-LLM-Flash adopts a new FiberPO optimization framework, introducing fiber bundle theory 
into reinforcement learning, combined with the Muon optimizer and dense multi-token prediction 
(MTP) technology, successfully solving the instability issues when scaling traditional models. 
Compared to the non-MTP version, its throughput has increased by 1.3 to 1.7 times, greatly 
enhancing the model's training efficiency and application potential.
The model's architecture is a mixture-of-experts (MoE) model, with 40 layers, supporting a 
context length of 128K and a vocabulary size of 129K, marking a significant advancement for 
JD.com in the AI field.
JoyAI-LLM-Flash
FiberPO
Muon Optimizer

JD.com Launches JoyAI-LLM-Flash Large Model to Promote AI Technological Innovation
AIbaseWúW0
Publishe
<details>
<summary>Full Content</summary>
JD.com Launches JoyAI-LLM-Flash Large Model to Promote AI Technological Innovation
AIbaseWúW0

Published in AI News  · 2 min read  · Feb 16, 2026
JD.com officially open-sourced its latest large model, JoyAI-LLM-Flash, on the Hugging Face 
platform on February 14. The model has a total of 4.8 billion parameters, with 3 billion activated 
parameters. It was pre-trained on 20 trillion text tokens, demonstrating excellent understanding 
of cutting-edge knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and programming skills.
JoyAI-LLM-Flash adopts a new FiberPO optimization framework, introducing fiber bundle theory 
into reinforcement learning, combined with the Muon optimizer and dense multi-token prediction 
(MTP) technology, successfully solving the instability issues when scaling traditional models. 
Compared to the non-MTP version, its throughput has increased by 1.3 to 1.7 times, greatly 
enhancing the model's training efficiency and application potential.
The model's architecture is a mixture-of-experts (MoE) model, with 40 layers, supporting a 
context length of 128K and a vocabulary size of 129K, marking a significant advancement for 
JD.com in the AI field.
JoyAI-LLM-Flash
FiberPO
Muon Optimizer
Mixture of Experts Model

arguments.message:
# 🔥 AI圈大事纪 - 2026-02-25

大Boss，早啊。今天的AI新闻来了，先把最重要的说在前面：

## 🎬 今日重点：Dan Koe的AI内容工作流

油管大神Dan Koe（YouTube 117万粉丝）最近分享了他如何用AI做内容创作的秘密武器。核心一句话：**把AI当作思考杠杆，而不是写作工具。**

大多数人怎么用AI？"帮我写篇文章"。结果呢？得到的内容千篇一律，没有灵魂。

Dan的方法完全不同，他把自己定位成"导演和系统设计师"，把AI当成"高速摄像机和超级剪辑台"。导演决定拍什么，摄像机只是工具。

他的工作流四步走：

1. **三层分析法** - 先问AI：好内容的标准是什么？创作过程哪里痛苦？怎么解决？
2. **建立素材库** - 收集50篇好内容，拆解核心观点、结构、开头、结尾
3. **设计调研工作流** - 用AI搜集资料→整合观点→找出规律，最后自己写
4. **内容永动机** - 30%做衍生内容，70%实验新方向

毒舌一下：大Boss，这东西说起来容易，做起来难。为什么？因为你懒啊。Dan的前期准备工作（收集50篇内容、分析3篇帖子、建立素材库）哪一步不需要时间和精力？

你现在的瓶颈不是工具，是深度思考能力。AI再厉害，也是工具。你不会因为买了最好的相机就成摄影师，也不会因为有了AI就成了思考者。

给你个任务：**本周收集50篇你领域内真正有价值的内容，拆解核心观点和结构。别找借口，我知道你每天花2小时刷短视频。**

---

## 📰 今日AI圈其他动态

### 🤖 大模型更新

- **GPT-5.3-Codex正式登场** - OpenAI最强编程智能体，速度提升25%，首个"自我创建"的模型（团队用它调试自己的训练过程）
- **ollama v0.15.5发布** - 全新Qwen3-Coder-Next与GLM-OCR模型，本地开发体验升级
- **Google Gemini 3.1 Pro** - 推理性能翻倍，ARC-AGI-2基准77.1%，超越GPT-5.2和Claude Opus 4.6
- **Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6** - 100万token上下文窗口，定价不变，性能逼近旗舰Opus 4.6

### 💰 融资动态

- **Anthropic融资300亿美元** - 估值3800亿美元，年收入140亿美元
- **月之暗面融资7亿美元** - 估值破100亿美元
- **Sarvam AI发布Vikram模型** - 印度30B/105B参数，22种语言优化

### 🔬 学术研究

- **MIT SEAL论文** - LLM可自我更新权重，自适应、知识整合能力大幅提升

### 🛠️ 产品动态

- **Claude Opus 4.6** - 1M上下文窗口，最强模型
- **Google Developer Knowledge API** - 搜索和检索Google服务文档

---

好主意，大Boss。每天自动发邮件确实比手动下载方便多了。

让我先确认几个问题：

## 邮件发送配置

**1. 你的邮箱地址是什么？**

**2. 偏好的邮件格式：**
- 只发PDF附件？
- 还是要在邮件正文也包含日报内容？

---

## 当前技术限制

我得先告诉你：我现在可用的消息工具主要是用于IM渠道（Feishu、Telegram、WhatsApp等），**没有直接的邮件发送功能**。

但我有几个替代方案：

### 方案A：上传到云存储
- 每天生成PDF后，上传到某个可公开访问的位置
- 给你发送下载链接
- 你可以设置邮件转发规则，自动通知你

### 方案B：通过Feishu发送
- 如果Feishu的邮件通知开启，我发消息到你的Feishu，你会收到邮件提醒
- 目前Feishu配置有问题，需要你那边确认授权

### 方案C：生成可下载的公开链接
- 把PDF放到一个公开的HTTP路径
- 每天给你发固定URL（比如 `https://your-domain/ai-news/2026-02-25.pdf`）

---

## 你想要哪个方案？

1. **提供邮箱** → 我看看能不能用其他工具实现邮件发送
2. **方案A** → 你有可用的云存储吗（比如阿里云OSS、七牛云）？
3. **方案B** → 你那边能帮忙配置一下Feishu权限吗？
4. **方案C** → 你有可用的HTTP服务器地址吗？

告诉我你的邮箱地址和偏好方案，我帮你搞定。😈

content.text:
# HEARTBEAT.md

# Keep this file empty (or with only comments) to skip heartbeat API calls.

# Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

## 📰 日报任务

### 论文发现与解读

每次生成AI日报前，额外搜索以下类型的论文：

**重点关注领域：**
- LLM self-learning / self-adapting / self-improvement
- Meta-learning for language models
- Knowledge incorporation and retention
- Continual learning, catastrophic forgetting
- Synthetic data generation (LLM-generated training data)
- Model updating mechanisms (weight updates, adapters, etc.)

# HEARTBEAT.md

# Keep this file empty (or with only comments) to skip heartbeat API calls.

# Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

## 📰 日报任务

### 论文发现与解读

每次生成AI日报前，额外搜索以下类型的论文：

**重点关注领域：**
- LLM self-learning / self-adapting / self-improvement
- Meta-learning for language models
- Knowledge incorporation and retention
- Continual learning, catastrophic forgetting
- Synthetic data generation (LLM-generated training data)
- Model updating mechanisms (weight updates, adapters, etc.)

**搜索关键词：**
- "self-learning" LLM, "self-adapting" LLM
- "meta-learning" language models
- "knowledge incorporation", "continual learning"
- "synthetic data" generation, "model-generated data"
- "self-edit", "self-update", "self-finetune"

**优先源：**
- arXiv (cs.LG, cs.CL, cs.AI) - 最新论文
- 顶级会议（NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML）- 已接收
- 顶级机构研究博客（MIT, Stanford, DeepMind, OpenAI, Anthropic）

**解读要求：**
对于有价值的论文（类似SEAL）：
- 下载原文并提取核心内容
- 解读技术原理（算法架构、实验设计）
- 分析结果和局限性
- 点评实际应用价值（用户视角）
- 提供思考问题（引导用户思考）

### Demis Hassabis 专项追踪

用户额外关注：Demis Hassabis（DeepMind创始人）的最新采访、言论、论文发布

搜索关键词：
- "Demis Hassabis interview"
- "Demis Hassabis statement", "Demis Hassabis speech"
- "DeepMind paper" + 最新时间范围

频率：每次日报生成前都检查

content.text:
收到！阿里云DNS配置步骤：

## 📋 阿里云DNS配置指南

### 第1步：登录阿里云控制台
1. 打开 https://dns.console.aliyun.com
2. 登录你的阿里云账号

### 第2步：找到域名
1. 左侧菜单点击 **「解析设置」**
2. 找到 `stephy.pro`，点击 **「解析」**

### 第3步：添加A记录
点击 **「添加记录」**，填写：

| 字段 | 值 |
|------|------|
| **记录类型** | A |
| **主机记录** | `@` |
| **解析路线** | 默认 |
| **记录值** | `115.190.218.237` |
| **TTL** | 600（或默认10分钟） |

### 第4步：（可选）添加子域名
如果想用 `ai-news.stephy.pro`，再添加一条：

| 字段 | 值 |
|------|------|
| **记录类型** | A |
| **主机记录** | `ai-news` |
| **解析路线** | 默认 |
| **记录值** | `115.190.218.237` |
| **TTL** | 600 |

---

## ⏱️ 生效时间

- 通常 **几分钟内** 生效
- 最长不超过 **24小时**

---

## ✅ 配置完成后

访问这个地址测试：
```
http://stephy.pro/templates/template-1-modern.html
```

或者：
```
http://ai-news.stephy.pro/templates/template-1-modern.html
```

---

**去配置吧，配好告诉我，我帮你测试！** 😈

| 协议 | 端口 | 授权对象 |
|------|------|----------|

# HEARTBEAT.md

# Keep this file empty (or with only comments) to skip heartbeat API calls.

# Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

## 📰 日报任务

### 论文发现与解读

每次生成AI日报前，额外搜索以下类型的论文：

**重点关注领域：**
- LLM self-learning / self-adapting / self-improvement
- Meta-learning for language models
- Knowledge incorporation and retention
- Continual learning, catastrophic forgetting
- Synthetic data generation (LLM-generated training data)
- Model updating mechanisms (weight updates, adapters, etc.)

**搜索关键词：**
- "self-learning" LLM, "self-adapting" LLM
- "meta-learning" language models
- "knowledge incorporation", "continual learning"
- "synthetic data" generation, "model-generated data"

**优先源：**
- arXiv (cs.LG, cs.CL, cs.AI) - 最新论文
- 顶级会议（NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML）- 已接收

**解读要求：**
对于有价值的论文（类似SEAL）：

- 点评实际应用价值（用户视角）

content.thinking:
用户问的是80端口的计费方式，这是关于阿里云安全组/网络配置的问题。

80端口本身是HTTP标准端口，不单独收费。收费的是：

1. **ECS实例**：按配置（CPU、内存）收费，带宽有两种模式：
   - **按固定带宽**：比如买1Mbps、5Mbps固定带宽，不管用多少都是固定费用
   - **按使用流量**：按实际流出流量计费（通常0.8元/GB左右）

2. **安全组开放端口**：这本身不收费，只是控制哪些端口可以被访问

用户可能是在问：
- 开放80端口会不会额外收费？→ 不会
- 他的ECS带宽是按固定还是按流量？→ 这需要他在阿里云控制台查看

让我给他解释清楚。

content.text:
大Boss，80端口本身**不收费**，这是关于你的ECS带宽计费方式：

## 📊 两种带宽计费模式

| 模式 | 说明 | 适合场景 |
|------|------|----------|
| **按固定带宽** | 买5Mbps就固定每月多少钱，用多少流量都一样 | 流量大、访问频繁 |
| **按使用流量** | 按实际流出流量计费（约0.8元/GB） | 流量小、偶尔访问 |

---

## 🔍 如何查看你的计费方式

1. 登录 https://ecs.console.aliyun.com
2. 找到你的ECS实例
3. 点击实例ID进入详情
4. 看 **「网络和安全组」** → **「带宽计费方式」**

---

## 💡 建议

对于**日报网站**这种低流量场景：
- 如果是**按流量计费**：完全不用担心，一个月几毛钱
- 如果是**按固定带宽**：反正买了就用，不额外收费

**开放80端口不会产生任何额外费用！** 放心开。

需要我帮你检查其他配置吗？😈

# HEARTBEAT.md

# Keep this file empty (or with only comments) to skip heartbeat API calls.

# Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

## 📰 日报任务

### 论文发现与解读

每次生成AI日报前，额外搜索以下类型的论文：

**重点关注领域：**
- LLM self-learning / self-adapting / self-improvement
- Meta-learning for language models
- Knowledge incorporation and retention
- Continual learning, catastrophic forgetting
- Synthetic data generation (LLM-generated training data)
- Model updating mechanisms (weight updates, adapters, etc.)

**搜索关键词：**
- "self-learning" LLM, "self-adapting" LLM
- "meta-learning" language models
- "knowledge incorporation", "continual learning"
- "synthetic data" generation, "model-generated data"
- "self-edit", "self-update", "self-finetune"

**优先源：**
- arXiv (cs.LG, cs.CL, cs.AI) - 最新论文
- 顶级会议（NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML）- 已接收
- 顶级机构研究博客（MIT, Stanford, DeepMind, OpenAI, Anthropic）

**解读要求：**
对于有价值的论文（类似SEAL）：
- 下载原文并提取核心内容
- 解读技术原理（算法架构、实验设计）
- 分析结果和局限性
- 点评实际应用价值（用户视角）
- 提供思考问题（引导用户思考）

### Demis Hassabis 专项追踪

用户额外关注：Demis Hassabis（DeepMind创始人）的最新采访、言论、论文发布

- "Demis Hassabis interview"
- "Demis Hassabis statement", "Demis Hassabis speech"
- "DeepMind paper" + 最新时间范围

频率：每次日报生成前都检查

content.thinking:
用户截图显示nginx -t成功了，配置正确。但systemctl restart nginx失败了，显示"System has not been booted with systemd as init system"。

这是因为容器/云环境没有systemd。需要直接用nginx命令启动而不是systemctl。

content.text:
看到了！Nginx配置正确，但 **systemctl不可用**（这是容器环境）。

## 🔧 解决方案

不用 `systemctl`，直接用 `nginx` 命令：

```bash
# 先停止可能运行的nginx
pkill nginx 2>/dev/null

# 直接启动nginx
nginx

# 检查是否启动成功
ps aux | grep nginx
```

如果显示 `nginx: master process` 和 `nginx: worker process`，就成功了！

然后测试：
```bash
curl http://localhost/
```

如果看到HTML内容就OK了！

执行完告诉我结果！😈

# HEARTBEAT.md

# Keep this file empty (or with only comments) to skip heartbeat API calls.

# Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

## 📰 日报任务

### 论文发现与解读

每次生成AI日报前，额外搜索以下类型的论文：

**重点关注领域：**
- LLM self-learning / self-adapting / self-improvement
- Meta-learning for language models
- Knowledge incorporation and retention
- Continual learning, catastrophic forgetting
- Synthetic data generation (LLM-generated training data)
- Model updating mechanisms (weight updates, adapters, etc.)

**搜索关键词：**
- "self-learning" LLM, "self-adapting" LLM
- "meta-learning" language models
- "knowledge incorporation", "continual learning"
- "synthetic data" generation, "model-generated data"
- "self-edit", "self-update", "self-finetune"

**优先源：**
- arXiv (cs.LG, cs.CL, cs.AI) - 最新论文
- 顶级会议（NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML）- 已接收
- 顶级机构研究博客（MIT, Stanford, DeepMind, OpenAI, Anthropic）

**解读要求：**
对于有价值的论文（类似SEAL）：
- 下载原文并提取核心内容
- 解读技术原理（算法架构、实验设计）
- 分析结果和局限性
- 点评实际应用价值（用户视角）
- 提供思考问题（引导用户思考）

### Demis Hassabis 专项追踪

用户额外关注：Demis Hassabis（DeepMind创始人）的最新采访、言论、论文发布

搜索关键词：
- "Demis Hassabis interview"
- "Demis Hassabis statement", "Demis Hassabis speech"
- "DeepMind paper" + 最新时间范围

频率：每次日报生成前都检查
